edges.description: Aberrant ATP release from mutant hemichannels disrupts normal keratinocyte differentiation and desquamation.
edges.source: Aberrant Hemichannel Activity and ATP Release

edges.description: Reduced gap junction-mediated intercellular communication contributes to impaired keratinocyte differentiation and hyperkeratosis.

nodes.description: The G11R and A88V mutations in Cx30 confer a gain of function, forming functional hemichannels at the cell surface that leak ATP into the extracellular medium. This aberrant paracrine signaling may alter the epidermal factors controlling keratinocyte proliferation and differentiation, contributing to the HED phenotype.
nodes.id: Aberrant Hemichannel Activity and ATP Release

evidence.reference_title: Connexin30 mutations responsible for hidrotic ectodermal dysplasia cause abnormal hemichannel activity.
evidence.snippet: we document a gain of function of G11R and A88V Cx30, which form functional hemichannels at the cell surface and, when expressed in HeLa cells, generate a leakage of ATP into the extracellular medium

evidence.reference_title: A recurrent mutation of GJB6 in a big Chinese family with Hidrotic ectodermal dysplasia.
evidence.snippet: Four mutations (p.G11R, p.A88V, p.V37E and p.D50N) in gap junction beta 6 (GJB6) gene, which codes connexin30 protein, have been found to cause HED in different populations

evidence.reference_title: Novel mutations in GJB6 and GJB2 in Clouston syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Mutations in the GJB6 gene, which encodes the gap junction protein connexin 30, have been shown to cause this disorder. To date, four mutations of GJB6 have been found in patients with CS: G11R, V37E, D50N and A88V

evidence.reference_title: GJB6, of which mutations underlie Clouston syndrome, is a potential direct target gene of p63.
evidence.snippet: We identified a recurrent heterozygous mutation c.31G>C (p.Gly11Arg) in the GJB6 gene in the Lebanese-German family with Clouston syndrome
GJB6 | HGNC:4288 | Clouston syndrome | MONDO:0007510 | AD | Definitive

nodes.description: In the absence of wild-type Cx30, skin disease-associated Cx30 mutations show impaired trafficking to the plasma membrane, preventing formation of functional gap junctions. This loss-of-function mechanism reduces intercellular communication in keratinocytes, complementing the hemichannel gain-of-function mechanism.

evidence.reference_title: Functional studies of human skin disease- and deafness-associated connexin 30 mutations.
evidence.snippet: For all three of the skin disease-associated Cx30 mutations investigated, impaired trafficking of the protein to the plasma membrane was observed thus preventing the formation of functional Cx30 gap junctions

nodes.description: Dysfunctional gap junction communication in keratinocytes disrupts normal epidermal differentiation and desquamation. Reduced intercellular coupling combined with aberrant hemichannel-mediated ATP signaling leads to hyperkeratosis, particularly in the palmoplantar epidermis where Cx30 is normally highly expressed.

evidence.reference_title: Connexin30 mutations responsible for hidrotic ectodermal dysplasia cause abnormal hemichannel activity.
evidence.snippet: Such increased ATP levels might act as a paracrine messenger that, by altering the epidermal factors which control the proliferation and differentiation of keratinocytes, may play an important role in the pathophysiological processes leading to the HED phenotype

evidence.reference_title: Phenotypic variability in gap junction syndromic skin disorders.
evidence.snippet: Moderate to severe hyperkeratosis is often present, with reduced keratinocytes desquamation

nodes.description: Second most common mutation, c.263C>T. Introduces a hydrophobic residue in the transmembrane M2 domain. Recurrently reported in Chinese Han families.

evidence.reference_title: A recurrent mutation of GJB6 in a big Chinese family with Hidrotic ectodermal dysplasia.
evidence.snippet: Sequence analysis revealed she and other 23 family members all carry a recurrent missense mutation p.A88V (c.263C > T) in GJB6

nodes.description: Novel mutation identified in a Han Chinese pedigree.

evidence.reference_title: Novel mutations in GJB6 and GJB2 in Clouston syndrome.
evidence.snippet: We found a novel missense mutation, N14S, in GJB6 and the previously identified F191L mutation in GJB2 (Cx26) in a proband with CS in a Han Chinese pedigree

nodes.description: Fourth known pathogenic mutation in GJB6 causing HED.
Most common mutation worldwide. Results from c.31G>C substitution in the N-terminus of Cx30. Found in French-Canadian, Polish, Lebanese-German, and other populations.

evidence.reference_title: Phenotypic variability in gap junction syndromic skin disorders: experience from KID and Clouston syndromes' clinical diagnostics.
evidence.snippet: a family with Clouston syndrome (caused by p.Gly11Arg mutation in GJB6), who are the first reported patients of Polish origin suffering from this disorder

evidence.reference_title: GJB6, of which mutations underlie Clouston syndrome, is a potential direct target gene of p63.
evidence.snippet: We identified a recurrent heterozygous mutation c.31G>C (p.Gly11Arg) in the GJB6 gene in the Lebanese-German family with Clouston syndrome

nodes.description: Novel heterozygous missense variant (c.134G>C) identified in a large Chinese pedigree with a unique phenotype including cold-triggered nail pain. Classified as likely pathogenic by ACMG criteria.

evidence.reference_title: A novel variant in the GJB6 gene in a large Chinese family with a unique phenotype of Clouston syndrome.
evidence.snippet: We found a novel heterozygous missense variant (c.134G>C:p.G45A) for Clouston syndrome

nodes.description: Less common mutation identified in HED families.
